transcripts.title: IELTS SPEAKING TEST 2017 - DESCRIBE YOUR NEIGHBORS

segments.text:
Hi everybody is Professor T!  This is an
IELTS Speaking Test question that's been

on the IELTS in 2017. They do change the
subjects and topics from time to time to

keep things fresh so here's one that's
been asked recently describe an

interesting neighbor and what makes a
neighbor interesting and what kind of

neighbor are you to your neighbors so
it's all about how we interact with

people who are our neighbors and here's
an example of how you could answer those

three questions I have a neighbor who's
from England so we're from a different

country he speaks the same language as
me but in a very different way so at

first we weren't really good friends
because there's some competition between

America my country and England but we
become friends over time and we share

more things we do like to joke around
about the difference in our accents the

difference in sports and politics but
it's light-hearted and fun as I got to

know this guy I learned that he liked to
read so I gave him some books that I

thought would be interesting to him plus
he has an interesting story since he was

a professional soldier and traveled all
over the world and he did a lot of

humanitarian work as well so I tried to
be a good neighbor by not making too

much noise but also in helping find out
what someone else needs and helping them

do it I helped this guy with his garden
some times and so if I go to town and

buy some special snacks I bring them
over for he and his wife just to be

friendly and neighborly those kind of
things make a good neighbour so I think

I'm a good neighbor because I try hard
to be respectful and I found things that

we have in common
and by being conscientious so that kind

of answer covers what is a good neighbor
and how you interact with your neighbors

in your hometown your neighbor might be
a family you

for a long time or it may be someone new
to the neighborhood you can describe

somebody in the neighborhood that's
interesting maybe there's an older

person who has had a lot of experience
in some type of business

maybe there's a cute little kid in your
neighborhood that you like to see grow

up or you have fun with maybe your
neighbors are your best friends or your

cousins that you've known for a long
time so there's always someone to talk

about who's in neighbor well maybe you
don't really know your neighbors some

people live in apartment buildings and
they don't really know their neighbors

because they only see them on the
hallway so you can describe something

like that and then describe the
characteristics of what a good neighbor

is and what a bad neighbor is and we
know how to be a good neighbor don't

steal anything
don't don't borrow a guy's car without

permission and try to be respectful in
terms of noise and so on so that's a

good answer for the IELTS  - the
questions are changed from time to time

to make it more interesting for the
IELTS examiner's and also to prevent

people from preparing too much for the
IELTS by memorizing an answer. What you

want to do is while you're listening to
the question take down some notes of

advantages and disadvantages what you do
and what you don't do and then when

you're giving your reply to the question
you'll have a very organized way of

delivering a great answer that explains
about your life and that's the most

interesting thing for an IELTS examiner
is to learn about someone else's life

and to hear a story that's well
organized and well told. So I wish you

good luck,  study hard and tell a great
story on the IELTS!

IELTS Speaking Test question that's been on the IELTS in 2017. They do change the

from England so we're from a different country he speaks the same language as

because there's some competition between America my country and England but we

about the difference in our accents the difference in sports and politics but

and by being conscientious so that kind of answer covers what is a good neighbor
and how you interact with your neighbors in your hometown your neighbor might be
a family you for a long time or it may be someone new
to the neighborhood you can describe somebody in the neighborhood that's
interesting maybe there's an older person who has had a lot of experience
in some type of business maybe there's a cute little kid in your
neighborhood that you like to see grow up or you have fun with maybe your
neighbors are your best friends or your cousins that you've known for a long
time so there's always someone to talk about who's in neighbor well maybe you
don't really know your neighbors some people live in apartment buildings and
they don't really know their neighbors because they only see them on the
hallway so you can describe something like that and then describe the
characteristics of what a good neighbor is and what a bad neighbor is and we
know how to be a good neighbor don't steal anything
don't don't borrow a guy's car without permission and try to be respectful in
terms of noise and so on so that's a good answer for the IELTS  - the
questions are changed from time to time to make it more interesting for the
IELTS examiner's and also to prevent people from preparing too much for the
IELTS by memorizing an answer. What you want to do is while you're listening to
the question take down some notes of advantages and disadvantages what you do
and what you don't do and then when you're giving your reply to the question
you'll have a very organized way of delivering a great answer that explains
about your life and that's the most interesting thing for an IELTS examiner
is to learn about someone else's life and to hear a story that's well
organized and well told. So I wish you good luck,  study hard and tell a great
story on the IELTS!

transcripts.title: IELTS Speaking Part 2: Describe a Childhood Neighborhood

segments.text:
Describe the neighborhood you lived in when you 
were a child. You should say we're in your town

or city. The neighborhood was: what kind of people 
lived there and what it was like to live in this

neighborhood. Also, explain whether you would 
like to live in this neighborhood in the future.
Alright, here's a sample answer:

When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood 
called Coin Pitch, which was situated on the

outskirts of our town. It was a peaceful 
and close-knit community nestled amid lush

greenery and rolling hills. The neighborhood 
primarily consisted of families and retirees,

creating a warm and friendly atmosphere. Living 
in Kangpi was truly a delightful experience.

The streets were lined with beautiful, 
well-maintained houses, each with its

own unique charm. The neighborhood 
had a strong sense of community,

and neighbors often engaged in activities 
together, such as organizing street parties,
picnics in the park, and holiday celebrations.

One of the standout features of this neighborhood 
was its emphasis on outdoor spaces. We had

numerous parks and playgrounds where children 
could freely play and explore. Additionally,

there were several walking trails that 
wound through the scenic landscape,

providing a perfect setting for 
outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers.

The residents of Kangpich were diverse yet 
unified. It was a multicultural neighborhood

where people from different backgrounds 
and cultures coexisted harmoniously.

This cultural diversity allowed for a 
rich exchange of ideas and traditions,

fostering an environment of 
acceptance and understanding.

Reflecting my experiences, I have cherished 
memories of growing up in Kangpich. The strong

community spirit and the safe environment made 
it an ideal place for a child to thrive. However,

as much as I hold dear the memories 
of my childhood neighborhood,

I wouldn't necessarily choose 
to live there in the future.

Although Kangpich offered tranquility and a 
close community, it lacked certain amenities

and opportunities that I seek in 
my adult life. As I have grown,

my preferences and priorities have shifted, 
and I now gravitate toward more vibrant urban

areas with better career prospects and 
a wider range of cultural activities.

So now let's get to the phrases and 
collocations and their simplified definitions:

Cozy neighborhood: A comfortable 
and pleasant area to live in.

Situated on: Located on the outskirts of.
Close-knit community: A tightly

connected and supportive group of people.
Lush greenery: Abundant and vibrant green plants.

Rolling hills: Hills that 
have gentle sloping shapes.

Creating a warm and friendly atmosphere: 
Making the area feel welcoming and pleasant.

A delightful experience: A very 
enjoyable and positive time.

Well-maintained houses: Houses 
that are kept in good condition.
Unique charm: Special and appealing qualities.

Sense of community: Feeling 
of belonging and togetherness.

Engaged in activities together: 
Doing things together as a group.
Street parties: Celebrations held on the street.

Picnics in the park: Eating 
meals outdoors in a park.

Holiday celebrations: Festivities 
and events during holidays.

Standout features: Particularly 
notable aspects or elements.

Outdoor spaces: Areas outside 
like parks and gardens.

Freely play and explore: Play and 
discover things without restrictions.
Walking trails: Paths for walking or hiking.

Wound through: Curved and twisted along 
a path. (This is the past form of "wind")

Scenic landscape: Beautiful 
and picturesque scenery.

Perfect setting: Ideal 
environments or surroundings.

Outdoor enthusiasts: People 
who enjoy outdoor activities.

Nature lovers: People who have 
a deep affection for nature.

Diverse yet unified: Different 
but together as a whole.

A multicultural neighborhood: A place 
with people from various cultures.

Coexisted harmoniously: Lived 
together peacefully and happily.
Cultural diversity: Variety of different cultures.

Rich exchange of ideas and traditions: Sharing and 
learning from each other's thoughts and customs.

Fostering an environment of acceptance and 
understanding: Encouraging and nurturing

an atmosphere of acceptance and understanding.
Reflecting on: Thinking back on or considering.

Cherished memories: Fond 
and special recollections.

Strong community spirit: A powerful 
sense of unity and togetherness.

Safe environments: Secure 
and protected surroundings.

An ideal place for: A perfect location for.
Hold dear the memories of:

Treasure and value the memories of.
Offered tranquility: Provided peace and calmness.

Close community: A tightly 
knit and intimate community.

Certain amenities and opportunities: Specific 
facilities and enhancements for progress.

Preferences and priorities: Personal 
choices and important matters.
Gravitate toward: Be drawn to or inclined towards.

Vibrant urban areas: Energetic 
and lively city regions.

Career prospects: Opportunities 
for professional advancement.

Wider range of: More diverse selection or options.
Cultural activities:

Events and experiences related to culture.
Alright, I hope you found it useful. Make

sure to practice using these phrases regularly 
to become more comfortable with them. This guide

is available for download, so you can keep it 
handy throughout your practice. Have a nice day!
